$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 14 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The king greeted the jewel era the way he greets everything: by mastering it first. One test, the paddock swears, was all Senna needed to find the MADONNA 456's new reference, and the whole circus spent the season studying the gap. The PALM 190 V10 sang in the compressed field like a soloist in a choir, Asselin kept the door with gloved calm, and the yellow-and-red garage treated the closest grid ever timed as a minor administrative detail. The Blink Season changed everyone's arithmetic except the #1's. The reference point, as ever, did not move.
team.madonna.objective: Master the jewel formula first, and let the closest grid in history study the gap.
team.madonna.definingEvent: Senna finds the Gen-5 reference in a single test, of course he does.
team.madonna.carryForward: The last climb is coming up the ladder at him, and the summit will not move for it.

team.firenze.summary: The wine-and-gold jewel drew gallery crowds to its launch, and then spent the season proving it was not merely pretty. Elssler, armed and silent, was closer to the crown than the grandees believed, the FIRENZE 500's FIRENZE 99 V12 finally in a formula that rewarded every alpine virtue he owned. Germi gave the tifosi silverwork Sundays. The house's one frustration was statistical: in a field covered by a blink, even perfection had company. Second to the crown, yes, but in the Blink Season the whole grid could see how short a distance second really was.
team.firenze.objective: Prove the wine-dark jewel is a weapon and not a gallery piece.
team.firenze.definingEvent: The launch-hall crowds come for the silverwork, and the season proves the engine is the exhibit.
team.firenze.carryForward: The ice is one compressed season from the interruption it has chased for a decade.

team.millions.summary: The invoice for the jewel era was paid loudly, in chrome. The MILLIONS 189 emerged from the winter as the grid's most expensively cut jewel, the DICK MD V10 polished to showroom standard, and the front-row census gave the gold garage exactly what it had always wanted: Saturdays worth buying. Alberti banked patrician arithmetic; Jones thrived in the blink, mugging front rows at maximum volume. The Blink Season's compression was new money's best argument: when everyone is within a heartbeat, a chequebook's worth of detail shows. The garage's mood was, for once, uncomplicated joy.
team.millions.objective: Buy the most expensive jewel on the grid, and make the blink a seller's market.
team.millions.definingEvent: The chrome Gen-5 tops its first Saturday, and the invoice is framed in the motorhome.
team.millions.carryForward: The gold has a car worthy of its noise, and the Jubilee ahead will give it a stage.

team.bestowal.summary: The cathedral's hoarded winter delivered: the BESTOWAL 167 emerged as the jewel formula's early reference at the fast circuits, the VAPOR DN's delivery finally mated to machinery drawn for it, and Blume's metronomic correctness found a car worthy of it. Picos kept the Sundays warm. The marble garage's only private worry was the same one everyone had: in a field covered by a blink, even German steel could be out-drawn on a bad Saturday. The benefactor, watching the closest grid ever timed, pronounced the money well spent, which at Bestowal is a love letter.
team.bestowal.objective: Make the hoarded winter pay, and be the fast circuit's reference in the jewel era.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The BESTOWAL 167 sets the jewel formula's early benchmark at the power circuits.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The cathedral has its car; now it needs the crown argument to go with it, and the clock is loud.

team.blanche.summary: The hour arrived. The white Gen-5 was genuinely, establishment-frighteningly quick, and Herbin's spotless craft became the reference lap the aristocrats pulled apart frame by frame, the whitecoats letting them. The BLANCHE 582's DELTA 103 V10 ran thrift and thunder at once, and the promotion dream stopped being spoken of as a joke anywhere on the big board. White grafted gloriously in support, still grinning from his second sunrise. The whitecoats' one concession to the moment was uncharacteristic: they allowed a small champagne into the garage. It was not opened. It was, everyone understood, only a matter of rounds.
team.blanche.objective: Turn the white Gen-5 into the establishment's problem, weekly and on merit.
team.blanche.definingEvent: Herbin's laps become the reference the aristocrats study, and the whitecoats let them look.
team.blanche.carryForward: The champagne is in the garage, unopened, and the whole paddock can see it sweating.

team.tyrant.summary: The Blink Season was boot-camp weather: a grid covered by a heartbeat is a grid where discipline moves mountains. Hamano's crossover patience put the TYRANT 548 onto front rows the gunmetal house had only ever threatened, and the LIZZIE 24 V8's reliability made the car a fixture at the finish of every close one. Gould found the walls unchanged by the jewel formula and preached at Canada on schedule. The boot camp's internal ledger noted the season's true profit: in a compressed field, intimidation by arrival was back on the menu, and nobody arrived like the #11 and #12.
team.tyrant.objective: Make the compressed field a boot-camp drill, and arrive everywhere at once.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Hamano puts the gunmetal car on a front row the house had only ever threatened.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The golden age will crown craftsmen too, and the blades intend to be measured first.

team.losel.summary: The year's head start paid in plain sight. While rivals learned the jewel formula by trial and error, the data door ran it by dossier, and Pacheco's LOSEL 125 was the season's overachiever, the #13 harvesting front-of-midfield positions with the VAPOR DNPQ V8's filed curves holding true. Dehehe closed his own sums as the Blink Season's tightest midfielder. The garage's only miscalculation was humility: it had modelled everything except how it would feel to be right this often. The archived conclusion from the family war, frost beats fire, was entered into the setup sheets, and the setup sheets won.
team.losel.objective: Cash the head start, and turn the jewel formula into a solved proof.
team.losel.definingEvent: The dossier beats the trial-and-error garages across a full compressed season.
team.losel.carryForward: The data door has the golden age modelled, and the model says Losel keeps climbing.

team.may.summary: Finishing power, spent on attack, exactly as promised. The jewel formula's reliability turned the leaf-green garage from gallant survivors into genuine muggers, Turner throwing the MAY 555 into fights the LORRY 32 V8 could now finish, and the front-row census included, to the paddock's delight, one green afternoon nobody had priced. The crew whistled louder than ever. The Blink Season's lesson, chalked on the garage wall beside last year's, read: hope is a strategy when the machinery holds. The youngest crew on the grid had grown up without noticing, and spring, it turned out, had teeth.
team.may.objective: Spend the new finishing power on attack, and put a green afternoon on the census.
team.may.definingEvent: The front-row census records one green afternoon nobody had priced.
team.may.carryForward: Spring has teeth now, and the golden age will be asked to notice.

team.bullets.summary: The second wind announced itself in autumn, exactly as the grandstands remembered it. The lock-up bolted in a customer LIZZIE 24 V8 that finally finished races, and the featherweight BULLETS 560, breathing again on customer-era reliability, started haunting the establishment's mirrors the way the old songs promised. Ceara drove like a man ten years younger and one winter from something. The garage had sold its first items to fund the season, quietly, without press. Nobody outside the lock-up yet knew how far the ledger would go. The insurgent was back in the conversation, and the conversation got nervous.
team.bullets.objective: Breathe again on customer-era reliability, and haunt the establishment's mirrors.
team.bullets.definingEvent: Ceara's second wind announces itself in the autumn rounds, and the big board notices.
team.bullets.carryForward: The ledger of things to sell is nearly full, and the last climb is fully funded.

team.dardan.summary: The oxblood house spent the jewel year watching its oldest enemy breathe again, and discovered the feeling was not hatred. The DARDAN 700 ran a worthy campaign in the compressed field, Alfven's ice and Bellini's late-blooming craft banking lower points with the VAPOR DNPQ V8 singing reliably, but the garage's eyes kept drifting to the backstreet mirrors where the sister feud used to live. The clan that had cheered Bellini at Italy now nodded at him in the pit lane, and the nod meant something. The grudge had one page left. Everyone at Dardan knew how it would end.
team.dardan.objective: Race the compressed field hard, and let the old grudge finish writing itself.
team.dardan.definingEvent: The garage catches itself nodding at the sister garage's revival, and does not stop.
team.dardan.carryForward: The last page of the feud will be written at Italy, with an open hand.

team.linden.summary: The elder simply transplanted. Moreau steered the LINDEN LN198 into the jewel era with the same unhurried green patience he had carried through two formulas before it, and the new formula's convergence only sweetened it. The LORRY 32 V8, seasoned like the workshop's own timber, refused to warp. The paddock set its watch by the old tree for a fourth dispensation. The garage's one concession to the jewel age was a new kettle. The Blink Season left the patient root closer to the front than in twenty years, and Moreau declined to mention it.
team.linden.objective: Transplant the root into the jewel era, and finish everything as always.
team.linden.definingEvent: The old green car runs its fourth dispensation, and the paddock keeps setting its watch.
team.linden.carryForward: The golden age has an elder statesman in green, and he is not done deepening.

team.minarae.summary: The flame caught the edge of the paper. Nono's menace became weekly fact in the Blink Season, the #21 flinging the MINARAE 594 onto front rows LEVEL C had no business visiting, and the SEGA SG1000 V8, flattered by the era's reliability, finally stopped being the excuse. Miller, arms folded, graded every lap and was seen, once, to smile. The forge's whole winter was aimed at next season, and the paddock could smell it. The jewel formula had compressed the ladder into a staircase, and the boy who ate hairpins for breakfast was climbing it two at a time.
team.minarae.objective: Make the menace weekly, and be ready to cash the cheque next season.
team.minarae.definingEvent: The #21 starts appearing on front rows LEVEL C has no business visiting.
team.minarae.carryForward: The forge is one season from arrival, and Miller has started drilling for it.

team.rigel.summary: The builders were measured, and the measurement came back kind. The museum's first original jewel-era bracket held a full season, and the RIGEL 3000, restored around it one final time, finished more races in the Blink Season than in any year of the Charter. Cotman banked his finishes like birthdays; Chardin held doors with a pride the courtesy had never quite carried before. The LORRY 32 V8 ran like the bright century it was born in. The lifers allowed the trophy shelf to catch the work-lamps a little longer each evening. The starlight, they agreed, was definitely getting closer.
team.rigel.objective: Prove the museum can build, and finish more races than any Charter year.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The first original bracket survives a full season, and the museum becomes a workshop.
team.rigel.carryForward: The starlight is close enough to steer by, and the golden age suits old stars.

team.comet.summary: The romantics got their almost-blaze. In the Blink Season's compressed pack, Tornio's silent COMET 323 was suddenly within a heartbeat of everything, and on one grey, perfect afternoon the black car ran in a lead group the timing screen had to be checked twice to believe. It lasted eleven laps. The garage talks about those eleven laps the way other garages talk about championships. The LIZZIE 24 V8 finished nearly everything. The jewel era had done what a decade of hope could not: put the comet back in the sky, briefly, and the whole pit lane looked up.
team.comet.objective: Catch one blaze in the compressed field, and make the pit lane look up.
team.comet.definingEvent: Eleven laps in the lead group: the black car back in the sky, briefly.
team.comet.carryForward: The garage now knows the sky is reachable, and the golden age has been warned.

team.orchis.summary: The flower bloomed in the open at last. The jewel era's gentler reliability and the survival-first doctrine turned the ORCHIS 056 from hothouse victim into the midfield's most elegant opportunist, Tegner reading skies and banking cool-afternoon harvests with a professor's calm. The MISFIRE 50 V8 completed its fourth clean season. The gardener-principal's ledger recorded the glasshouse's finest year of the modern age, modest by the big board's measure, miraculous by his. The frost gambler's doctrine had been vindicated by engineering: a flower that survives everything does, in fact, bloom anywhere.
team.orchis.objective: Bloom in the open, and make the survival doctrine pay in the golden age.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The glasshouse records its finest year of the modern age, modest and miraculous.
team.orchis.carryForward: The flower has a doctrine and a future, and the frost gambler intends to harvest both.

team.zeroforce.summary: The classified year. The customer LORRY 32 V8 turned the ZEROFORCE 231 from a walking home into a finishing car, and Klinger, the grid's most patient man, reached the flag so often the chalk wall ran out of room. The whole pit lane watched the #32 like a favourite serial, and the toast for the rare points day stood poured and waiting in every garage on the tour. The gallows humour had a new problem: it was running out of material. The end of the road, the crew admitted over slightly better coffee, had become a place with a view.
team.zeroforce.objective: Finish everything the customer engine can finish, and earn the toast the pit lane has poured.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: The chalk wall runs out of room, and the pit lane starts standing by with glasses.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The points day is coming, and twenty-three garages intend to be there when it lands.

team.joke.summary: The carnival's answer to the most beautiful cars ever built was to be gloriously, deliberately ugly among them, and the Blink Season loved it. Dufay's fake-strategy theatre reached its artistic peak in a compressed field, the JOKE 777 appearing on front rows it had no business visiting and refusing to explain how. The POND V8 ran like a tramp in a gallery, somehow always there at the end. The ledger held in LEVEL B, the grandstands howled, and the jewel era discovered that beauty is only half a show. Somebody has to throw the cream pie. The #16 volunteered.
team.joke.objective: Be the ugly masterpiece in the jewel gallery, and keep the blink honest.
team.joke.definingEvent: The #16 crashes the jewel era's front rows and refuses to explain how.
team.joke.carryForward: The golden age will need a jester at its jubilees, and the carnival is already fitted.

team.lares.summary: The hearth kept its one promise in a brand-new era: the car came home. The jewel era's convergence suited the LARES 92 beautifully, Arai's courtesy placing the hearth-red car in close finishes the horsepower years would have swallowed, and the RAM V12's manners made it the grid's most dependable Sunday companion. The mantel filled steadily. The paddock, busy falling in love with the pretty new machinery, kept one warm corner of its heart for the garage that never changes, and the lamp stayed lit. The jewel era, the house noted, was just another season to be faithful in.
team.lares.objective: Bring the car home in the golden age, exactly as in every age before it.
team.lares.definingEvent: The hearth-red car finishes the Blink Season's closest packs, faithful as the lamp.
team.lares.carryForward: The golden age will test every garage's faith, and the hearth's is already proven.

team.feet.summary: The jewel era was built by hand somewhere, and the grafters made sure part of it was theirs. The FEET 13 went into the winter with two tools and came out with a third, pace, sweat-built and sweat-understood, that let Rampal fight in the pack rather than merely survive it. The YOUGEN V10 answered the era's reliability with interest. Old Boots came home in the points with a regularity that stopped being remarkable and started being expected, which at Feet is the highest honour going. The pit lane's caps stayed tipped, with something new in the gesture: respect.
team.feet.objective: Add the third tool, pace, and fight in the pack instead of surviving it.
team.feet.definingEvent: The points stop being remarkable and start being expected, the garage's highest honour.
team.feet.carryForward: The golden age respects sweat, and the grafters are owed a season of it.

team.serga.summary: The grey car watched the floor breathe and felt the parable proving itself. With the jewel era's reliability reaching even the last garage, Sambena's creed, weld it, drive it, finish it, stopped being a curiosity and became a template. The SERGA 1000's SC3000 F12 completed another honest season, the thousand small names bright on the flanks. The mountain man heard that a bigger garage down the road was planning something like his whole life at national scale, and smiled into his welding. The thanks, as ever, were already on the car. Soon, he suspected, they would be on another.
team.serga.objective: Keep the template honest, and watch the parable prove itself across the floor.
team.serga.definingEvent: The creed becomes the floor's template, and the mountain man smiles into his welding.
team.serga.carryForward: The national retelling is coming, and the original will watch it with greasy hands.

team.cool.summary: The prettiest show on earth, watched from the best seats in it. The jewel cars were everything the deckchairs had hoped, gorgeous, close and loud, and Delvaux chased the light among them with a connoisseur's contentment, the COOL 05's CORSE V8 humming its sunset tune in a field full of orchestras. The Blink Season meant the garage's festivals came closer together, and one golden evening the #28 finished in a pack that took the tannoy three reads to sort. The light went gold over the most beautiful machinery ever built, and the glass was raised.
team.cool.objective: Enjoy the golden age from the deckchairs, and catch the light where it falls.
team.cool.definingEvent: One close-pack finish takes the tannoy three reads to sort, a festival.
team.cool.carryForward: The golden age will only get prettier, and the deckchairs are permanently placed.

team.moon.summary: The something real arrived, and the orbit closed on it. The jewel era's convergence and reliability reached the far side of the grid like daylight, and the MOON 292, with Yepes waving and grinning and finishing, stopped being the paddock's distant dot and became the back of its pack. The RAM V12 completed the season with a completeness the chalk wall recorded in disbelief. The low voices permitted a sentence never before spoken in the silver garage: the midfield is visible from here. The telescope stayed up. Every moon is on the rise, and this one had started to wax.
team.moon.objective: Close the orbit onto the back of the real pack, and finish the season entire.
team.moon.definingEvent: The sentence is spoken in the silver garage: the midfield is visible from here.
team.moon.carryForward: The waxing has begun, and the golden age will have to make room for one more body.

team.iris.summary: The fire's deadline ticked into a new era, and the violet house answered with silverwork. Iris's cut-glass Gen-5 drew gallery crowds and went hunting, Salgado throwing the IRIS 717 into the Blink Season's front-row lottery with the vow riding on every lap. The PRISM 90 V10 sang at the razor's edge; the white gloves kept everything immaculate in a season that punished elegance with contact. The flame stayed among the armed all year, the second crown still one reach ahead, the clock still audible. Across the wall, the frost kept pace, and the fire refused to find that motivating.
team.iris.objective: Hunt the second crown through the blink, with the vow riding on every lap.
team.iris.definingEvent: The cut-glass jewel launches to gallery crowds, and the hunt goes public in a new era.
team.iris.carryForward: The clock is now the loudest thing in the cathedral, and the fire races it first.

team.azalea.summary: The frost wintered at the front, exactly as planted. Larssen's cold consistency made the AZALEA 808 a fixture in the Blink Season's compressed lead pack, the #34 banking the kind of arithmetic that turns a bet into a favourite, and the rooftop lung gave Mexico its annual masterclass in thin air. The BLOOM 88 V8 never missed a beat. The glasshouse's decade of cultivation was now openly a crown argument, and the garage responded with its signature move: nothing. The bloom holds, the frost waits, and the sister house's fire, burning loud across the wall, was treated as weather.
team.azalea.objective: Turn the bet into a favourite, and hold the bloom at the front all season.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The #34 becomes a fixture of the compressed lead pack, and the frost is a favourite.
team.azalea.carryForward: The golden age has a cold heart now, and the glasshouse is owed a crown argument.
